description: Can you recover the secret XOR key we used to encrypt the flag?
name: My Magic Bytes

description: This encryption service will encrypt almost any plaintext. Can you abuse the implementation to actually encrypt every plaintext?
name: The impossible user

description: We RSA encrypted the flag, but forgot to save the private key. Is it possible to recover the flag without it?
name: Suspicious Caesar Cipher

description: We put together a substitution-flag-permutation network. We encrypted the flag with the network, but forgot to write down the key. Can you reverse the network and recover the flag plaintext?
name: Substitution-flag-permutation network

description: We XOR encrypted this file, but forgot to save the password. Can you recover the password for us and find the flag?
name: An exclusive key

description: This encryption service will encrypt the flag along with any plaintext, but it won't decrypt it. Can you recover the flag anyway?
name: Hidden flag bytes

description: We are trying to save time by limiting our calls to random. Can you abuse the predictable encryption mechanism to recover the flag?
name: Predictable vectors

description: Can you abuse the flag HMAC implementation and forge a valid request?
name: The flag HMAC

description: We ran out of time to implement encryption for our cipher. Can you abuse the decryption implementation to recover the flag?
name: Nonexistent functionality
